title: Chainswap signature or quorum validation failure historical bridge incident

historical_exploit.attack_summary: The cross-chain bridge Chainswap announced the details of the stolen incident on its official blog. A total of 20 project assets were stolen, with a total value of approximately US$4 million. At present, the ChainSwap team has reached a consensus with the affected projects and initially formulated and implemented a compensation plan. According to the project investigation, due to the error in the token cross-chain quota code, the on-chain swap bridge quota is automatically increased by the signature node, the purpose of which is to be more decentralized without manual control. However, due to a logical flaw in the code, this led to a vulnerability that automatically increases the number of invalid addresses that are not whitelisted.
historical_exploit.attacker_profile: External attacker or unprivileged caller as described by the SlowMist incident row; exact actor permissions must be re-verified before reporting.
preconditions: A bridge, cross-chain router, gateway, wrapped-asset minter, liquidity pool, or validator/proof path accepts attacker-controlled message, proof, signature, transaction, deposit, configuration, or accounting input.
The accepted input releases existing bridge-held value, mints unbacked wrapped assets, miscredits a deposit, or lets a caller move assets beyond entitlement.
The target mapping must be reachable without relying on privileged key compromise, DNS/frontend phishing, social-account compromise, or pure rug-pull behavior.

value_path.asset_or_reward: bridge-held assets, wrapped assets, bridge liquidity, withdrawals, refunds, or backed cross-chain liabilities
value_path.how_value_was_extracted: The cross-chain bridge Chainswap announced the details of the stolen incident on its official blog. A total of 20 project assets were stolen, with a total value of approximately US$4 million. At present, the ChainSwap team has reached a consensus with the affected projects and initially formulated and implemented a compensation plan. According to the project investigation, due to the error in the token cross-chain quota code, the on-chain swap bridge quota is automatically increased by the signature node, the purpose of which is to be more decentralized without manual control. However, due to a logical flaw in the code, this led to a vulnerability that automatically increases the number of invalid addresses that are not whitelisted.
value_path.why_it_paid: The historical row describes real loss, attempted bridge theft, unbacked minting, unauthorized withdrawal, or bridge-accounting failure that can be useful only if BridgeIndex has a matching current live-value extraction path.

mapping.why_this_might_map: BridgeIndex exposes bridge deposits, bot-gas accounting, withdrawal creation/approval/execution, refunds, token/resource mappings, relayer voting, and proposal execution. This historical primitive is useful only if it maps to extracting current BNB/USDT ledger value or backed bridge liabilities from the deployed BridgeIndex system.

notes: Selected for scanned because: ChainSwap bridge contract vulnerability
This seed is not proof that BridgeIndex is vulnerable; Workflow 7 must reject it unless the primitive maps to non-admin extraction of a current live ledger row.
